reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #fog-feel-tuning-live-user-eye #e2e-literal-sync-on-render-constant: the long-carried fog-feel tuning (waiting since S57 for the live user eye) resolved in one Micro pass once the user gave concrete direction during the 3-tab smoke: FOG_COLOR + MEMORY_FOG_COLOR to 0x000000 (pure black = bg, kill the blue tint) + R_PERSONAL 150->75 (halve the spark reveal). KEY GOTCHA (the standing e2e-outside-tsconfig warning bit exactly here): e2e/fog.spec.ts HARDCODES the fog color literal {r:5,g:7,b:13} and pixel-asserts it; tsc stayed green but Playwright would have silently failed; had to sweep + sync the literal to {0,0,0} and RUN playwright. The e2e became the runtime arbiter: test 1 extracts the mask pixel and now asserts concealed=(0,0,0), proving fog renders pure black AND stays opaque (concealment intact), stronger than a screenshot for a color question. Lesson: a render-constant change must sweep e2e specs for hardcoded literals of that constant; when an e2e already pixel-extracts what you changed, updating its literal makes it self-prove the new value. Also: preview_screenshot hangs on a continuously-rendering Pixi/WebGL canvas (eval stays instant), so rely on pixel-sample eval + e2e for objective proof and the user live look for aesthetic.

P2 #nplayer-verify-two-layer #extract-pixels-render-arbiter #adversarial-check-extracts-real-flaw: verified FFA to 6 in two layers - deterministic unit (applyStartGame N=4/5/6 + palette guard, the seating LOGIC) + runtime e2e (4-peer real-WebRTC netcode incl green@seat3, + N=6 single-page deterministic RENDER proof). KEY: Council DROPPED flaky 6-peer real (15 channels/swiftshader) for a deterministic N=6 render proof; the adversarial CHECK (Grok FAIL) then caught that my N=6 proof was a FALSE PASS (world-state + no-pageerror does not prove the renderer DREW the 6 colors) - FIXED by adding app.renderer.extract.pixels(stage) asserting all 6 palette colors appear on the canvas (same arbiter e2e/fog.spec uses). Lesson: for un-unit-testable Pixi, a screenshot is human-only; the CI-grade render proof is a pixel-extract color-presence assertion. Also: hardcoded e2e color literals (e2e cannot import src) need a src-pinning unit canary so a palette change fails loud.

P3 #lobby-construction-net #behavior-net-over-internals #cover-before-refactor: built the Playwright net the S61 Council required BEFORE the deferred 548-LOC lobbyScreen refactor - single-page deterministic coverage of the high-risk state machine (DOM input + sanitiser wiring, host->room-code, Begin-Match gating via updatePeerStatus, reset clears). KEY: a BEHAVIOR net (assert observable surfaces via __SPARK__ accessors + DOM) is the correct refactor guard - you refactor internals, the net catches behavior breakage - so Grok-ANALYST CONDITIONAL constructor-not-exercised was REJECTED (the constructor IS exercised via its outputs; reaching LOBBY + clickable HOST proves wiring). The real multi-peer Begin trigger is covered by the 3/4-peer e2e, so driving updatePeerStatus directly here is correct layering not tautology. Lesson: cover-then-refactor for an un-unit-testable Pixi/DOM component = a deterministic single-page e2e over the public accessors; log the uncovered modes (pane-switching, joining/error, listener cleanup) as the refactor session net-extension scope.

SESSION #method #worked #autonomy-pivot: on AFK+autonomy, defer EYE-DEPENDENT items (visual tweaks/refactor, CVD aesthetics) as logged carry-forwards and execute ONLY the verification-safe core; the absent-user adversarial Council/CHECK stands in for the missing eye (S61 precedent re-validated; Grok ANALYZE 8.7/10 - better to ship zero eye-dependent than low-confidence).

SESSION #method #worked #pixel-arbiter-not-screenshot: for un-unit-testable Pixi render, the CI-grade proof = app.renderer.extract.pixels color-presence assertion + a src-pinning unit canary for the duplicated e2e literals + N-run flake-stability; a human-only screenshot is NOT a CI proof (the adversarial CHECK caught a screenshot-only N=6 render as a FALSE PASS). Institutionalize.
